items.title: NST Penang former stringer dies
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Former New Straits Times Penang reporter Paul Toh Huat Hock, 77, has died. K. Kandiah, 74, a former sports contributor, said he was shocked to receive news about Toh's passing. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Graft trial of Spanish PM's ex-top aide begins
items.summary: MADRID: A corruption trial of Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's former right-hand man that has rocked the government opened on Tuesday, with the defendant's ex-partner among the first witnesses to testify. The trial of Jose Luis Abalos, a former Socialist heavyweight and transport minister who helped propel Sanchez to power in 2018, is the first of several corruption affairs shaking the fragile minority coalition to reach trial. The case has embarrassed Sanchez, who came to power vowing to clean up Spanish politics after ousting a conservative Popular Party (PP) government in a no-confidence vote over its own graft scandal.

items.title: Rafizi treading Azmin path, warns PKR branch chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli's recent criticisms of both PKR and the government mirror actions once associated with former party deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali, a party leader has claimed. Pasir Mas PKR branch chief Mohd Izhan Ramli said Rafizi's tone, approach and pattern of public statements have gone beyond ordinary internal criticism, instead reflecting developments that previously led to internal fractures within the party. He said Rafizi, a former PKR deputy president, had previously assured party leaders and delegates that he would not undermine the party from within or betray the trust placed in him, particularly in relation to prime minister and party president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

items.title: Arrest warrants out against Daim Zainuddin’s two sons and their associate for skipping MACC investigation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The Magistrate’s Court today issued arrest warrants against three individuals, including two sons of the late Tun Daim Zainuddin, for failing to comply with notices from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to face charges today. Magistrate Illi Marisqa Khalizan made the ruling after allowing an application by Deputy Public Prosecutor Mahadi Abdul Jumaat for arrest warrants to be issued against Muhammed Amir Zainuddin, 31, Muhammed Amin Zainuddin, 28, and businesswoman Josephine Premla. The prosecution’s application has merit, and therefore, the arrest warrants are issued,” she said.

items.title: B100 pilot shows promise at RM4.50 per litre, Felda plans wider rollout to boost energy security
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — B100, a 100 per cent palm oil-based biodiesel, has the potential to emerge as a more competitive and sustainable alternative energy source amid uncertainties arising from the West Asia crisis, said Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek. Ahmad Shabery said he has brought up the matter with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. B100’s factory price is estimated at about RM4.50 per litre, depending on CPO prices, Ahmad Shabery said.

items.title: From housewife to lawyer: Woman without SPM finds purpose after son’s murder charge ordeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A woman’s life changed dramatically after her son was charged in a murder case, prompting her to pursue a law career despite not having completed Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM). Azlina Abdul Aziz, 52, said the 2016 ordeal marked a turning point in her life. Azlina said she was a full-time housewife until her son’s arrest in 2016.

items.title: WFH push could cut fuel use by 30pct, ease subsidy burden: Economist
items.summary: But stronger conservation measures are still needed to manage fuel consumption and subsidy costs, he added Williams said petrol and diesel use should be reduced and managed more efficiently to stretch supplies and curb the rising subsidy bill. Williams warned that if fuel subsidy spending rises to RM60 billion, it would become a major fiscal burden, exceeding the RM46.5 billion allocated for public healthcare. "Import prices may be affected because higher oil prices push up prices of imports.

items.title: U Mobile kicks off 5G rollout with TM under three-year wholesale deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: U Mobile Sdn Bhd has kicked off work with Telekom Malaysia Bhd following a three-year 5G wholesale agreement, marking a key step in supporting the latter's network rollout. The agreement, secured after a structured evaluation process, will see U Mobile support TM's 5G rollout under a multi-operator core network arrangement as part of its convergence strategy. "We are on track to deliver the 5G network to TM," chief technology officer Woon Ooi Yuen said after unveiling the company's Ultra 5G solutions portfolio.

items.title: Ministry to roll out 5,000 projects to boost urban well-being
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: More than 5,000 public infrastructure projects will be rolled out nationwide this year to upgrade public amenities, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said. Nga said the initiatives are part of several priorities set by the ministry to strengthen sustainable urban development. On waste management, Nga said the ministry is targeting the development of 50 smart recycling centres this year, in addition to the existing 133 drive-through recycling centres.

items.title: Central banks risk losing another battle by trying to win the last war
items.summary: ON the face of it, the Middle East energy crisis is precisely the sort of external shock that central banks should "look through." In theory, central banks should avoid changing policy in relation to this event unless it's clear that it will persist or boost inflation expectations long term. Central banks may not heed this advice.

items.title: FMM urges immediate government action over Hormuz disruption
items.summary: FMM said raw material shortages, rising logistics costs and tightening diesel supply are threatening production continuity across sectors producing food, household goods, packaging, chemicals and consumer products. About 48.6 per cent of companies reported increases of between 10 per cent and 30 per cent, while 21.8 per cent reported increases of between 30 per cent and 50 per cent. A further 11.8 per cent reported increases exceeding 50 per cent.

items.title: Johor Perhilitan seizes RM36mil in wildlife parts
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Wildlife and National Parks Department's (Perhilitan) found a cache of animal parts worth more than RM36 million at a house in Taman Johor Jaya here. Perhilitan director-general Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said the premises was inspected in the presence of the suspect, revealing a haul of wildlife contraband: 602 suspected python gall bladders, 420 items believed to be bear bile, 107 compressed pieces suspected to be tiger parts, 135 compressed pieces thought to be from primates, and 191 segments suspected to be python reproductive organs. The case is being investigated under the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 (Act 716), specifically Sections 60 and 68 for possession of wildlife parts without valid authorisation.

items.title: CIQ congestion causes lorries to wait for hours at Tanjung Kupang
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Facilities for heavy vehicle inspections at the Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex in Tanjung Kupang, Iskandar Puteri, are struggling to cope with the thousands of lorries entering Singapore via the Linkedua route. The congestion, which has been ongoing since mid-February, has caused anxiety among lorry drivers who have been dealing with long waiting times. Lorry driver Aidil Osman, 34, said each lorry entering Singapore must pass through a scanning machine, which takes nearly five minutes per vehicle.

items.title: Kajang man jailed six months after throwing kitten against laundry wall
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A 49-year-old man who went viral for throwing a kitten against a wall at a self-service laundry was sentenced to six months in jail today after failing to pay a RM60,000 fine imposed by the Sessions Court here. Judge Khainur Aleeza Ismail had earlier sentenced Toh Teck Hooi to the fine and stipulated that failure to pay would result in six months’ imprisonment, according to Harian Metro . The next day, Selangor JPV Animal Welfare officers inspected the site.

items.title: All universities corrupt? One or two cases is too broad stroke, says UTM chief
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 7 — Claims that higher education institutions (IPT) are breeding grounds for corruption have been called irresponsible, as they risk undermining and tarnishing the reputation of the country’s education system. Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) vice-chancellor Prof Dr Mohd Shafry Mohd Rahim said that although there have been isolated cases, they cannot be used as a basis to label all IPTs. Mohd Shafry said every government agency or organisation, including universities, has clear integrity and governance systems, including in procurement processes.

items.title: Protesters briefly disrupt Azam Baki’s keynote address at WTC in Kuala Lumpur (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A keynote address by Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki was momentarily interrupted when two men entered a hall with banners during the Wacana Ilmiah Perdana programme at the World Trade Centre (WTC) here. According to a report in The Star , witnesses said that one of the men shouted and rushed towards the front of the stage, causing a brief commotion among attendees. Despite the disruption, Azam continued his speech professionally, even making a lighthearted remark to ease tension.

items.title: Dr M denies causing Malay divisions, blames Umno changes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former prime minister Tun Dr Mahathir Mohamad has denied accusations that he caused divisions among Malays, saying the split was driven by changes within Umno. He said he later formed Bersatu to provide an alternative platform for Malays disillusioned with Umno, with the aim of maintaining unity under a single party. Dr Mahathir acknowledged that divisions among Malays persist, but stressed that unity rather than any single party has always been the source of strength, citing how Malays once rallied together under one organisation to achieve political success.

items.title: Police seek public help to locate missing woman
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: Police are seeking public assistance to locate a woman who was reported missing after leaving her home on Thursday (April 2). Seberang Perai Selatan (SPS) Police chief Supt Jay January Siowou said the 38-year-old woman, G. Veaknes, was reported missing by her family. "Anyone with information on her whereabouts is urged to contact Nibong Tebal Police Station chief Insp Mohd Muzamir Ghazali at 017-5100056, or to notify the nearest police station," he said in a statement today.

items.title: No special circumstances: Federal Court denies Zuraida’s bid to revisit PKR bond reduction
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 7 — The Federal Court here today dismissed former PKR vice-president Datuk Zuraida Kamaruddin’s application to review its earlier decision granting PKR leave to appeal against the appellate court’s ruling to reduce the bond payment from RM10 million to RM100,000 in a party bond breach case. On June 24, last year, the Federal Court granted PKR leave to appeal against the matter. On Dec 11, 2024, the appellate court three-man bench led by Judge Datuk See Mee Chun upheld the High Court’s ruling that Zuraida had breached the bond but reduced the quantum payment from RM10 million to RM100,000.

items.title: Arrest warrants issued against Daim Zainuddin's sons
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The magistrate's court today issued arrest warrants against two sons of the late Tun Daim Zainuddin for failing to comply with a notice by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to appear in court to face charges. The duo are Muhammed Amir Zainuddin, 31, and Muhammed Amin Zainuddin, 28. Magistrate Illi Marisqa Khalizan also issued the same warrant against businesswoman Josephine Premla, 65.

items.title: Iran says ‘one by one’ for passage of Malaysia’s six remaining ships stuck at Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The remaining six Malaysian vessels stranded in the Strait of Hormuz will be cleared to pass through the waterway in stages, Iran’s Ambassador to Malaysia Valiollah Mohammadi Nasrabadi said. Earlier, the Malaysian Foreign Ministry confirmed that one of the seven Malaysian-owned vessels previously stranded in the Strait of Hormuz due to the evolving regional situation had been granted passage and was proceeding to its final destination. Iran has allowed passage through the Strait of Hormuz to vessels from “non-hostile” nations, affirming that it will facilitate to ensure their safe passage.

items.title: Train driver killed, 27 people injured as French TGV collides with truck
items.summary: LILLE: The driver of a high-speed TGV train was killed and 27 people injured in France today when the train collided with a truck, officials said. The accident occurred at a level crossing between the towns of Bethune and Lens in the northern French region of Pas-de-Calais at around 7:00am, said rail operator SNCF. The SNCF said that rail services would be suspended between Bethune and Lens until at least late today.

items.title: Woman charged over offensive posts on Malaysian, Brunei royal institutions
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A single mother pleaded not guilty at the Sessions court today to two counts of making and initiating offensive communications online last month. Nurul Huda Mohd Bajuri, 49, made the plea after the charges were read before Judge Mohd Azhar Othman. Deputy Public Prosecutor Mohamad Afdhallan Syafiq Azmi requested bail at RM15,000 for both charges with two local sureties.

items.title: Corruption rooted in systemic weaknesses, not just crime, says Azam Baki
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Corruption in Malaysia is not merely a legal offence but a consequence of deeper systemic weaknesses in governance. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said while laws are necessary to punish offenders, only strong integrity embedded within institutions and society can effectively prevent corruption. He said Malaysia is now at a critical stage where strengthening governance and institutional integrity must become central to national development, particularly as the country matures economically.

items.title: Overnight attacks kill children in Ukraine, Russia
items.summary: MOSCOW: Two boys were killed in Russia and Ukraine overnight, officials said Tuesday, as the two sides exchanged latest strikes more than four years after Moscow sent troops into its neighbour. As a result of the latest strikes, in Russia a boy born in 2014 was among three people killed in the Vladimir region after a drone struck a residential building, the regional governor said. In Russia's Vladimir region, two adults and their son, born in 2014, were killed, governor Alexander Avdeev said on Telegram, adding that the couple's five-year-old daughter was in hospital with burns.

items.title: Defence mega projects to go ahead despite energy crisis as delays could hike costs, says deputy minister
items.summary: LUMUT, April 7 — Mega projects under the Ministry of Defence will continue as planned, including the Royal Malaysian Navy’s (RMN) Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project, despite the ongoing global energy crisis. Deputy Defence Minister Adly Zahari said the projects will proceed according to the schedule outlined in the 13th Malaysia Plan, as they are critical to national security and sovereignty. Meanwhile, Adly said the ministry will identify cost-saving measures to be implemented without affecting the Malaysian Armed Forces’ (MAF) operations and training.

items.title: Malaysia used ties with Iran to unblock vessels in Strait of Hormuz - Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has leveraged its trusted ties with Iran to help resolve the blockage of Malaysian commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said his close interactions with Iranian leaders allowed him to convey Malaysia's concerns directly and secure assurances for prompt action. "Several of our major vessels were stranded following Iran's temporary closure of the strait in response to actions by the United States and Israel.

items.title: No criminal elements in UPSI bowling centre storeroom death, say police
items.summary: TANJUNG MALIM: Police have confirmed that no criminal elements were involved in the death of a 53-year-old man at a bowling centre at Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) early today. Muallim district police chief Superintendent Khalid Mohamad said the victim, a local man who worked as a cleaner at the premises, was found unconscious in a utility room. "At 1.57am, the district police headquarters operations room received information via the NG999 system regarding the incident.

items.title: Storm alert: Seven states plus Labuan brace for heavy rain, strong winds until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected over seven states, as well as the entire Federal Territory of Labuan, until 5 pm today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said such weather conditions are expected in Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar and Batang Padang), Kelantan (Jeli, Tanah Merah, Machang, Pasir Puteh, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang), Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Hulu Terengganu, Dungun and Kemaman), Pahang (Tanah Tinggi Cameron, Lipis, Jerantut, Kuantan, Pekan and Rompin) and Johor (Tangkak, Muar, Mersing, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru). Sarawak will also be affected, involving Sarikei (Meradong), Sibu (Sibu dan Selangau), Mukah, Kapit (Song, Kapit dan Bukit Mabong), Bintulu (Tatau dan Sebauh) and Limbang, while Sabah, involving Pedalaman (Sipitang, Kuala Penyu, Beaufort and Tambunan), as well as the west coast areas.

items.title: Chef pleads guilty to raping girlfriend's daughter
items.summary: Deputy public prosecutor Norazihah Asmuni did not offer bail, pending sentencing. The court allowed bail at RM10,000 and fixed June 15 for the submission of a social report and sentencing. Accused Muhammad Amirullah Fadly Osman, 19, pleaded not guilty.

items.title: Madani Marts are owned by entrepreneurs; Yayasan Madani licensor for branding
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Yayasan Madani has clarified that Madani Mart operates on a public-private-social partnership (PPSP) model, with outlets fully owned by entrepreneurs and no government funds involved in its implementation. Its manager, Lutfi Mohd Ariffin, said the foundation takes serious allegations surrounding the initiative, particularly those related to sources of capital, ownership and governance. This ensures that every Madani Mart is a locally owned business, not owned by the government or the foundation," he said.

items.title: Govt revives plan for National Harmony Commission
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is reviving plans to establish an independent National Harmony Commission, with the National Unity Ministry backing a renewed proposal amid growing concerns over unity-related issues. It said the move follows a proposal by Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye and builds on earlier efforts dating back to 2015, when the National Unity Advisory Council drafted three bills on racial and religious hatred offences, national harmony and reconciliation, and the establishment of a commission. kw: National Harmony Commission, unity, inter-ethnic relations, inter-religious harmony, JPNIN, mediation, reconciliation, legislation, nst, news, Malaysia © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Defence Ministry launches autism support programme for children of personnel
items.summary: LUMUT: The Defence Ministry today launched the Malaysian Armed Forces Autism Initiative (IA-ATM), a programme aimed at providing therapy and support for children of defence personnel with autism. Its deputy minister, Adly Zahari said IA-ATM is initially being implemented at two locations, at the Community-Based Rehabilitation Organisation (PPDK) Lautan Kasih in Lumut and PPDK Kem Terendak in Melaka, involving 20 children. "This programme focuses on children with autism aged three to 15 years who are children of defence personnel.

items.title: Online gaming platforms expose children to grooming risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An acquaintance that began through an online game ultimately led to requests for inappropriate images. What initially started as a casual conversation became risky when the individual began requesting inappropriate images, before it was discovered by her parents. Child clinical psychologist Dr Noor Aishah Roslan said most children today already own smartphones and have access to social media from a young age, yet their level of maturity has not fully developed to assess risks.

items.title: [UPDATED] Protesters disrupt Azam Baki's speech at public forum
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A public address by Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki at the Wacana Ilmiah Perdana was briefly disrupted by three youths who staged a protest during the event. He moved to the middle of the hall and began shouting "Tangkap Azam Baki", interrupting the event. #kw: Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission, MACC, chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam, disruption, protest, Wacana Ilmiah Perdana, speech © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Malaysia to push for South China Sea code with China - Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia will push for a regional code of conduct with China to resolve disputes in the South China Sea without outside interference, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Speaking at the Kuala Lumpur–Ankara Dialogue 2026, Anwar acknowledged ongoing tensions involving China and the Philippines but stressed that Asean must take the lead in managing the disputes. ends./ #kw, Malaysia, News, Nst, Malaysia News, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Nation,South China Sea, Asean, CoC SLUG: ANWAR74 Malaysia to push for South China Sea code with China - Anwar By Hakim Mahari hakimmahari@nst.com.my With pix ASWADI ALIAS KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia will push for a regional code of conduct with China to resolve disputes in the South China Sea without outside interference, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.

items.title: Post-Raya delivery volumes hit new highs, says Lalamove
items.summary: Post-festivities, delivery volumes have rebounded within five working days, according to on-demand delivery platform Lalamove, signalling a consistent return to business activity despite prolonged holiday periods. Lalamove's data over the past three years shows that the time taken for post-Raya delivery volumes to return to pre-festive levels has remained unchanged, while the scale of post-Raya delivery demand has grown steadily year-on-year. Lalamove's delivery demand, previously dominated by food and beverage (F&B) orders in 2024, has diversified to include sectors such as auto parts, construction materials and machinery.

items.title: Hotspots remain under control amid hot spell, said Zahid
items.summary: SUBANG: Fire hotspots nationwide remain under control, with only isolated cases detected as the country continues to experience a hot spell, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. Zahid, who is also chairman of the National Disaster Management Committee, said there were currently no major fire hotspots, with only minor fire-affected areas detected. Other areas, such as Johor, have been observed from time to time, but overall the number is low," he said at a press conference at Subang Air Base.

items.title: Cloud seeding operations produce rain in Perlis and Kedah
items.summary: SUBANG: Cloud seeding operations have begun showing results, with slight improvements recorded as efforts continue to induce rainfall in targeted areas of Peninsular Malaysia. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the operations, which began yesterday based on advice from the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), brought rainfall to Perlis and Kedah, starting at the Muda Dam and spreading across other target locations. The government has launched cloud seeding operations to increase water levels at key dams amid ongoing hot weather.

items.title: Selective application of rules-based order undermines global stability - Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A rules-based international order cannot be applied selectively, as double standards risk undermining global stability and national sovereignty. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said such a system's credibility depends on rules being applied consistently and fairly, especially during times of crisis. A rules-based international order cannot be invoked selectively.

items.title: Malaysian palm oil producers want Putrajaya to match Asean peers with stronger biodiesel use
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The Malaysian Biodiesel Association (MBA) has called on the government to follow the proactive measures announced by other Asean counterparts and expedite the nationwide implementation of higher blends under the national biodiesel programme for both the transport and industrial sectors. The association said that Indonesia and Thailand have announced measures to further advance their national biodiesel programmes by increasing biodiesel blending levels. MBA believed that, by leveraging Malaysia’s status as a leading palm oil producer, the government should further encourage the use of biodiesel outside the national biodiesel blending programme by exempting the 10 per cent sales tax on biodiesel.

items.title: Ipoh man, 25, dies in Buntong machete brawl; cops nab one suspect, looking for two more
items.summary: IPOH, April 7 — A 25-year-old man was found dead with slash wounds while some of his friends were injured in a machete attack in the Buntong area here yesterday. Ipoh police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said they received information about an argument at about 11.10 pm and dispatched a team to the scene. “Upon inspection, we found a man lying on the road with severe injuries from a sharp weapon.

items.title: Widow claiming royal connections lands in dock over obscene posts on TikTok, Instagram targeting Sultan Ibrahim and Brunei Ruler
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 7 — A 49-year-old single mother who claimed to be a teacher to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim was charged with sending obscene messages insulting Malaysian and Brunei royalty today. Nurul Huda Mohd Bajuri pled not guilty after the two charges were read out before Sessions Court Judge Mohd Azhar Othman. On the first charge, Nurul Huda was accused of knowingly transmitting obscene messages via TikTok under the account Ratu Nur Putri Ratu Balqis, where she claimed to be a teacher to Sultan Ibrahim, the Johor ruler, as well as other state monarchs, with the intention of upsetting others.

items.title: Kelantan abuse-of-power claims? ROS says 2017–2018 complaints investigated and closed in same year
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 7 — Investigations into complaints of misconduct and abuse of power involving several organisations in Kelantan received in 2017 and 2018 were resolved in the same year in accordance with the provisions under the Societies Act 1966, according to the Registrar of Societies Malaysia (ROS). In a statement today, the ROS said the investigation included the examination of related documents and representations from several involved parties, with appropriate enforcement actions taken against the relevant organisations in accordance with existing legal provisions, in addition to official notifications being conveyed to the complainants regarding the investigation results. “The ROS received written complaints regarding financial management and organisational governance issues in 2017 and 2018.

items.title: Like, share, earn? Bukit Aman warns Malaysians after RM251m lost to online part-time job scam since Jan 2025
items.summary: He said that a total of 10,304 cases were recorded during that period, involving losses of approximately RM251.2 million, with Selangor recording the highest number of cases and losses, namely 2,684 cases involving losses of RM80.18 million, followed by Kuala Lumpur with 1,170 cases (RM36.47 million) and Johor recording 1,443 cases (RM27.31 million). “For the period from Jan 1 to March 15, the age group of 31 to 40 years recorded the highest number of victims, with 435 cases, followed by the 21 to 30 years group (388), 41 to 50 years (289), 15 to 20 years (108), 51 to 60 years (100), and 61 years and above (56).” Commenting further, he said that women comprise most of the victims, with 6,734 people in 2025 compared to 2,194 men, whereas in the early period of 2026, 930 women were recorded compared to 446 men. “Civil servants also recorded 717 victims, followed by retirees (320 people), business people (370), teachers (245), private clerks (244), government clerks (62), and the security sector (eight victims).

items.title: Malaysia looks to Singapore, Australia, Zimbabwe for new harmony and reconciliation Bill, eyes mediation over punishment
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 7 — The drafting of the National Harmony and Reconciliation Commission (SKRN) bill, led by the Department of National Unity and Integration (JPNIN), will focus on preventive and conciliatory measures. The Ministry of National Unity (KPN) said the move comes in response to recent developments and growing controversies over unity issues that have threatened social harmony. “The ministry will ensure the commission can investigate issues affecting social harmony, summon relevant parties to give evidence, and issue recommendations and preventive measures to the government and relevant agencies,” the statement added.

items.title: Penang quit rent for 12ha plot slashed from RM334,544 to RM180, owners urged to appeal by April 30
items.summary: Major cuts confirmed: Penang has reduced quit rent rates by 78.5 to 82 per cent, including a case where a 12-hectare plot dropped from RM334,544 to RM180 after review. Appeals key to savings: Landowners must file appeals by April 30 to benefit from the reductions; the RM50 appeal fee has been waived. More changes in pipeline: The state is considering further measures, including a 50 per cent rebate this year and revised rates for low-cost housing, villages, and non-profit land use.

items.title: ‘Not guilty’ plea from ‘boss.s_k’ Instagramer charged with posting offensive content online
items.summary: SEPANG, April 7 — A trader pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to charges of creating and posting offensive content via social media two years ago. Sanuzi Kernain, 42, the accused, entered the plea after the charge was read before Judge Ahmad Fuad Othman. MCMC prosecutor Nazrul Nizam Mohd Zameri proposed bail of RM10,000 with one surety.

items.title: Anwar to urge Asean, China to fast-track South China Sea Code of Conduct at May summit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said he will urge his regional counterparts and China to expedite the conclusion of Code of Conduct (COC) on the South China Sea at the Asean Leaders’ Summit in Philippines next month. During the 2025 Asean Summit in Kuala Lumpur last October, Anwar said Chinese Premier Li Qiang has agreed to expedite talks on the COC. Asean and China signed the non-binding Declaration on the Conduct of Asean and China (DOC) in 2002 and initiated talks for a legally-binding COC in 2013.

items.title: Penang plans new state administrative centre, legislative assembly on Silicon Island
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 7 — The Penang government is planning the construction of a new state administrative centre including a new state legislative assembly on Silicon Island. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the Penang Development Corporation (PDC) was appointed to assess spatial and design requirements from various state agencies. He said data collection is underway to enable detailed architectural and land use planning for the administrative centre.

items.title: Former Johor property executive pleads not guilty to RM1m CBT charges
items.summary: Rafidah Abdul Hamid, 48, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read out before Judge Datuk Che Wan Zaidi Che Wan Ibrahim. According to the charge sheet, the accused is charged with committing criminal breach of trust by fraudulently using money, amounting to between RM73,000 and RM317,610, for her own benefit. while the accused unrepresented.

items.title: Land for Silicon Island LRT depot set for handover in May, says Penang CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 7 — Land for the Light Rail Transit (LRT) depot on Silicon Island is expected to be handed over to MRT Corporation Sdn Bhd (MRTC) as early as May this year, said Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. Chow said Silicon Island represents more than just a land reclamation project but a long-term commitment by the Penang government to future generations. An artist’s impression of Penang Silicon Island.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia’s fuel supply secure until June, thanks Iranian president for safe transit of ships via Hormuz
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Malaysia has sufficient fuel supply to meet local consumption needs until June, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim reiterated today. Speaking at the Kuala Lumpur-Ankara Dialogue 2026, Anwar credited national oil giant Petronas for building robust ties with other oil producing nations that has ensured adequate fuel supply for Malaysia despite the ongoing war in West Asia. Anwar also expressed his gratitude to Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian for promising the safe passage of Malaysian ships carrying crude oil across the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: UPSI bowling centre employee found dead had complained of headache, says manager
items.summary: Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) Sports Bowl manager Dzulkarnain Sapian said the employee was only found around 1am after his wife came to the bowling centre to look for her husband, who had not returned home after finishing work last night. “The victim had complained to his wife last evening about having a headache. Dzulkarnain said the victim, Mohd Radzi Ariffin, 53, was a cleaner at the private company, UPSI Sports Bowl, which operates within the UPSI campus.

items.title: Trailer driver charged with murder over Johor crash that killed three family members
items.summary: SEGAMAT, April 7 — The trailer driver involved in the fatal crash that claimed the lives of three family members at KM212 of the Johor Bahru–Seremban Road, near the Taman Golden Hill junction in Gemas Baharu last week, was charged at the Magistrate’s Court here today. The accused, Muhamad Shafiq Salleh, 29, nodded in understanding as the charge was read before Magistrate Amalina Johar. Earlier, Transport Minister Anthony Loke directed the Road Transport Department (JPJ) and the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD) to conduct an immediate audit and investigation into the trailer operator.

items.title: Anwar backs Turkiye’s call for restraint in West Asia, praises Egypt, Oman and Pakistan mediating US-Iran tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said Malaysia fully backs Turkiye’s advocacy for all parties to exercise restraint in the West Asia war that erupted over a month ago. Stressing the need for engagement over escalation, the prime minister also expressed concerns that the ongoing war could potentially fuel a new arms race in an already-volatile region. Anwar said international law should be advocated and applied consistently in order for a rules-based order to have credibility.

items.title: Singaporean professionals fined RM1,000, ordered community service for littering cigarette butts outside Johor mall
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 7 — Two Singaporean professionals were fined RM1,000 each and ordered to perform six hours of community service by the Sessions Court here today for throwing cigarette butts in a public place. Lim, an architect, and Tan, a project manager, were ordered to complete six hours of community service at a rate of two hours per day within a period not exceeding six months. The Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Act 2007 came into force nationwide on January 1.

items.title: Seremban Jaya assemblyman’s childhood home goes up in flames, family safe in dinner outing
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 7 — The family home of Seremban Jaya assemblyman Datuk P. Gunasekaran was destroyed in a fire at Kampung Baru Rahang here last night. Gunasekaran said his late parents and three siblings used to live in the house, bought around 1990. “This old house holds many memories.

items.title: Fadhlina vows thorough probe, accountability after IPG kayaking fatalities in Perlis
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 7 — The Ministry of Education (MoE) is reviewing the standard operating procedures (SOPs) for outdoor activities across all its institutions to strengthen safety measures. The review comes after an accident at Pantai Merdeka last Saturday, which claimed the lives of two trainee teachers from the Institute of Teacher Education (IPG) Perlis Campus during an off-campus programme. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the ministry takes full responsibility for the tragedy.

items.title: Police refer Balik Pulau assault case to DPP after outcry over no charges
items.summary: Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the investigation paper is being prepared for submission. “The case is being investigated under Section 323 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt, Section 506 for criminal intimidation and Section 427 for mischief,” he said in a statement issued today. “The victim received treatment at Balik Pulau Hospital,” he said.
